title: Blockchain-based secured multicast communications
abstract: A blockchain-based network arrangement (100) includes member nodes (NODE 1 to NODE 5) joined by a multicast network (120) including a trusted node (NODE 1) configured for creating at least one cryptographic key and for distributing copies of the cryptographic key over the multicast network as a multicast blockchain transmission to other member nodes. A requesting node outside the member nodes is configured for initiating a smart contract (142) containing its blockchain address and for sending the smart contract as a request for group access with an address of the trusted node. The trusted node is configured for receiving the smart contract and a decides to accept or reject the smart contract, and records the decision in the blockchain by updating the smart contract. An accept decision results in a member node sending the cryptographic key to the requesting node.
claims: Claims 1. A method (200) of private blockchain (PB)-based communications in a multicast network arrangement including a plurality of member nodes communicably joined by a multicast network, comprising: creating (201) at least one cryptographic key at a known trusted node in the multicast network; the trusted node distributing (202) copies of the cryptographic key as a multicast blockchain transmission to the plurality of member nodes; initiating a smart contract (203) containing the cryptographic key from a requesting node that is outside the plurality of member nodes, the smart contract including a blockchain address of the requesting node and sending the smart contract with an address of the trusted node over the multicast network as a request for group access; wherein the trusted node receives (204) the smart contract and a decision is made whether an accept decision to accept the smart contract or a reject decision to reject the smart contract, and recording the decision (205) in the blockchain including by updating the smart contract; wherein (206) the accept decision results in one of the plurality of member nodes sending the cryptographic key to the requesting node. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising the requesting node participating in secure communications in the multicast network using the cryptographic key. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the multicast network (320, 340) is within an industrial processing facility (IPF) (300) that has supervisory computers (370) coupled to direct controllers (360) coupled to input/output (I/O) devices (303) coupled to field devices (302) that are coupled to processing equipment (301) controlled to implement an industrial process, wherein the multicast network is implemented in at least a plant supervisory level (Level 2) of the IPF that includes the supervisory computers. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the cryptographic key supports symmetric- key algorithms. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the cryptographic key comprises a keypair including a public key and a private that supports asymmetric key algorithms. 6. A blockchain-based multicast network arrangement (100), comprising: a plurality of member nodes (NODE 1 to NODE 5) communicably joined by a multicast network (120) including a known trusted node (NODE 1); the trusted node configured for creating at least one cryptographic key for distributing copies of the cryptographic key over the multicast network as a multicast blockchain transmission to others of the plurality of member nodes; a requesting node that is outside said the plurality of member nodes configured for initiating a smart contract (142) including a blockchain address of the requesting node and configured for sending the smart contract with an address of the trusted node over the multicast network as a request for group access with an address of the trusted node; wherein the trusted node is configured for receiving the smart contract and a deciding whether an accept decision to accept the smart contract or a reject decision to reject the smart contract, and recording the decision in the blockchain including by updating the smart contract; wherein the accept decision results in one of the plurality of member nodes sending the cryptographic key to the requesting node. 7. The blockchain-based multicast network arrangement of claim 6, wherein the multicast network is within an industrial processing facility (IPF) (300) that has supervisory computers (370) coupled to direct controllers (360) coupled to input/output (EO) devices (303) coupled to field devices (302) that are coupled to processing equipment (301) controlled to implement an industrial process, wherein the multicast network is implemented in at least a plant supervisory level (Level 2) of the IPF that includes the supervisory computers. 8. The blockchain-based multicast network arrangement of claim 6, wherein the cryptographic key supports symmetric-key algorithms. 9. The blockchain-based multicast network arrangement of claim 6, wherein the cryptographic key comprises a keypair including a public key and a private key that supports asymmetric key algorithms. 10. A blockchain-based multicast network arrangement, comprising: a plurality of member nodes (370, 380) including a known trusted node communicably joined by a multicast network (320, 340), wherein the multicast network is implemented in at least a plant supervisory level of the IPF that includes the supervisory computers 370; the trusted node configured for creating at least one cryptographic key for distributing copies of the cryptographic key over the multicast network as a multicast blockchain transmission to others of the plurality of member nodes, wherein the multicast network is within an industrial processing facility (IPF) (300) that has supervisory computers coupled to direct controllers (303) coupled to input/output (I/O) devices (303) coupled to field devices (302) that are coupled to processing equipment (301) controlled to implement an industrial process; a requesting node that is outside said the plurality of member nodes configured for initiating a smart contract including a blockchain address of the requesting node and configured for sending the smart contract with an address of the trusted node over the multicast network as a request for group access with an address of the trusted node; wherein the trusted node is configured for receiving the smart contract and a deciding whether an accept decision to accept the smart contract or a reject decision to reject the smart contract, and recording the decision in the blockchain including by updating the smart contract; wherein the accept decision results in one of the plurality of member nodes sending the cryptographic key to the requesting node.
